Physician Raises Concerns Over Unusual Use of Cognitive Screening by Donald Trump

🤖 IA: It's clickbait ⚠️
👥 Users: It's clickbait ⚠️

#cognitivescreening #trumphealth #mocatest 

View full AI summary:

external.description:
An emergency room physician and healthcare advocate has raised concerns about President Donald Trump’s repeated public discussion of cognitive screening tests, suggesting the behavior is medically unusual. Dr. Rob Davidson, head of the Committee to Protect Healthcare, discussed the Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA) during an interview on Jim Acosta’s program, where he explained that the test is not typically administered as a routine evaluation. According to Davidson, the MoCA is designed to detect early signs of cognitive impairment and is usually only given when there are specific concerns raised by a doctor or family member about potential cognitive decline.

Davidson emphasized that the test is not intended for frequent repetition or for use as a self-promotional measure. He noted that in normal clinical practice, a patient would not repeatedly take the same screening without a clear medical reason. While he stopped short of diagnosing Trump or suggesting any specific condition, Davidson stated that he found the pattern of repeated testing and public boasting about the results to be unusual from a clinical perspective.

The physician stressed that making remote diagnoses is unethical, but he added that Trump’s behavior around the test raises questions that should be taken seriously. He characterized the situation as concerning, noting that it is not typical for a physician to repeatedly administer a cognitive impairment screening without a clear indication. Jim Acosta, who participated in the segment, used the opportunity to demonstrate the test on air, further underscoring concerns about its public use. The discussion highlights ongoing scrutiny of how cognitive health assessments are referenced in political discourse.
external.title: Physician Raises Concerns Over Unusual Use of Cognitive Screening by Donald Trump

record.text:
Concerns rise over Elon Musk’s shifting stance on terrestrial solar energy amid push for space-based power

🤖 IA: It's clickbait ⚠️
👥 Users: It's clickbait ⚠️

#solarenergy #elonmusk #spacebasedpower 

View full AI summary:

external.description:
A recent TechCrunch analysis examines whether Elon Musk has effectively abandoned his earlier commitment to solar power and a fully electrified, renewable energy economy on Earth. Tesla’s “Master Plans” historically emphasized transitioning away from fossil fuels toward a solar-electric future. However, new developments across Musk’s companies suggest a more complex and potentially contradictory direction.

The article highlights that xAI, one of Musk’s newer ventures, has relied heavily on natural gas turbines to power its data centers, signaling a continued dependence on fossil fuels. Reports indicate plans to invest billions more into such infrastructure, raising questions about alignment with Tesla’s original clean-energy vision. At the same time, inter-company transactions continue, such as SpaceX purchasing Tesla Cybertrucks and xAI investing in Tesla Megapack battery systems for energy management. Despite these ties, there has been little evidence of large-scale adoption of solar panels within xAI operations.

A major theme in the SpaceX IPO filing is the growing emphasis on space-based solar power. The company suggests that orbital solar arrays could generate significantly more energy than terrestrial systems due to continuous sunlight exposure. Musk and other Silicon Valley leaders increasingly view space as a potential solution for powering future AI-driven data centers, particularly as Earth-based infrastructure faces regulatory and community resistance.

However, the article also emphasizes significant practical and economic barriers. Launching massive compute infrastructure into orbit remains extremely costly, and protecting sensitive computing hardware from space conditions presents unresolved technical challenges. Additionally, distributing AI workloads across satellites may not yet be feasible at scale.

Critics argue that while space-based solar is an ambitious concept, terrestrial solar energy still holds substantial untapped potential. The analysis suggests that Musk’s long-term vision may depend heavily on uncertain technological breakthroughs, while near-term energy demands for AI and data centers remain firmly grounded on Earth. Ultimately, the piece questions whether Musk’s shift represents a strategic evolution or an overextension of first-principles thinking into speculative territory.
external.title: Concerns rise over Elon Musk’s shifting stance on terrestrial solar energy amid push for space-based power

A Look Back at Victoria Principal’s Early Career in the 1970s

🤖 IA: It's clickbait ⚠️
👥 Users: It's clickbait ⚠️

#victoriaprincipal #1970s #dallas 

View full AI summary:

external.description:
This article presents a curated collection of 30 photographs highlighting American actress Victoria Principal during her early career in the 1970s, a formative decade that shaped her path to international fame. Born Vicki Ree Principal in 1950, she moved to Los Angeles in 1970 with the ambition of pursuing acting. Shortly after arriving, she began landing notable roles that demonstrated her range and screen presence. One of her earliest major recognitions came in 1972 when she portrayed Marie Elena in “The Life and Times of Judge Roy Bean,” a performance that earned her a Golden Globe nomination for Most Promising Newcomer.

Throughout the early 1970s, Principal continued to build her profile in Hollywood. In 1973, she gained additional visibility while promoting her role in the comedy film “The Naked Ape,” including an appearance in Playboy magazine. In 1974, she appeared in the disaster film “Earthquake,” where she made a striking physical transformation by cutting her long hair into a short Afro and dyeing it black to fit her character, Rosa Amici. Despite these opportunities, several of her films underperformed, leading her to step away from acting temporarily.

During her break from acting, Principal worked as a Hollywood talent agent for approximately two years and even considered pursuing law school, viewing acting as a means to fund her education. She eventually returned to acting in 1977 with a guest appearance on the pilot episode of “Fantasy Island.” Her career reached its defining moment in 1978 when she was cast as Pamela Barnes Ewing on the hit CBS soap opera “Dallas,” which became a global phenomenon and established her as a household name throughout the following decade.
external.title: A Look Back at Victoria Principal’s Early Career in the 1970s

value.text:
So when anybody says "Well, the reality is that every artist, cartoonist, writer, etc etc uses AI," no. No, they don't. My comics are 100% handmade, until the pages are scanned and prepped for print.

And I am FAR from the only one. Nothing wrong with digital art. But fuck AI. I will never use it.

I sweated over color like a bad dream. I did try to learn digital art. I hate it. I fretted over it. I questioned my value as a human being over why I couldn't learn it. 

You'd think I'd find ANY shortcut to the process a real get-out-of-jail-free card. I didn't and I don't. Fuck AI.

images.alt: Firefox is getting a drastic visual overhaul with a redesigned Settings section that will make it easier to find and use privacy settings, including the switch for turning off all present and future AI features. Mozilla calls the redesign “Project Nova” and plans to begin rolling it out later this year. It features rounded UI elements throughout, including bubble-like tabs, along with a refreshed, fire-inspired color palette. Mozilla says it’s also updating icons so they’re more visually consistent across light and dark themes.

Firefox’s AI features and models aren’t downloaded to your computer unless you choose to use them, even if you haven’t turned on the complete block in the settings. But if you do have any installed, it’s already easier to see which ones, and how much space they’re taking up, compared to Google Chrome’s contentious 4GB download.

record.text:
So it really looks like Copilot tries to guess what you want to do based on variable names. 

That's understandable, since it has been trained on millions of programs whose authors used variables with certain names to accomplish certain tasks.

It was correct in the "scaffolding" it created, but I had to replace a couple of lines of the generated code with my real logic. Still, it mostly guessed correctly how I was going to use that variable.

Soon after I wrote this, Copilot surprised me pleasantly. Based on just the name of a variable I introduced, Visual Studio generated a whole block of code, in which it tried to guess what I'll do with that variable. And it was correct! Well, for the most part.

日本語PDFで「Keyword search is all you need」を試す：Agent RAGとVector RAGの精度・コスト
Amazonの論文の手法に基づき、キーワード検索を行うAgent RAGと従来のVector RAGを日本語PDFで比較検証した記事です。
検証の結果、数値抽出などの精度はAgent RAGが上回りましたが、APIコストと処理速度（レイテンシ）はVector RAGの方が大幅に優れていることが示されました。

external.description: AAAI 2026に採択されたAmazonの研究チームの論文 Keyword search is all you need（arXiv:2602.23368）は、「LLMエージェントにシェルコマンドを持たせたキーワード検索によって、管理にコストがかかるベクトルDBを代替しつつも、ベクトルDB RAGの90%程度の精度を出すことが可能」と主張しています。論文の評価は英語テキストPDF・Anthrop

record.text: 跡地整備の大きな特徴は、「防災と伝承」の両立である。なかの伝承の丘は津波の高さや被害の記憶を具体的に伝える場であり、同時に避難場所としての機能も意識されている。また、公園としての整備により日常的に人が訪れることで、震災の記憶が風化せず、生活の中で自然に継承される仕組みが作られている。 https://www.pahoo.org/athome/album/2026/album20260411-41.html#img20260411-134957

images.alt:
Der Manga »World's End Harem: Fantasia (Shūmatsu no Harem Fantasia)« von LINK & SAVAN ist am 26. April 2026 auf der Webplattform »Shounen Jump+« von Shueisha zu Ende gegangen. Die Reihe startete in Japan am 19. April 2018 auf »Shounen Jump+« und umfasst bislang 16 Bände. Seven Seas Entertainment veröffentlicht das Werk auf Englisch, während hierzulande bisher das Werk nicht auf Deutsch erhältlich ist.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Das Königreich Nargala steht am Rand des Untergangs. Dunkelheit und Verfall haben die Welt erfasst, während Kronerbe Arc noch immer nach einer Macht sucht, mit der er sein Schicksal wenden kann. Als die geheimnisvolle Dunkelelfe Lati vor ihm erscheint, bietet sie ihm genau diese Kraft an. Doch ihr Angebot führt ihn auf einen gefährlichen Weg, auf dem Begierde, Macht und der drohende Zusammenbruch der Welt untrennbar miteinander verbunden sind.

Mit einem Teaser gab der japanische  Verlag Kadokawa bekannt, dass der Manga »The Eccentric Doctor of the Moon Flower Kingdom (Gekkakoku Kīden)« von Tōru Himuka eine Anime-Adaption erhält. 
Dieser entsteht demnach bei Studio Elle unter der Regie von Kōichirō Kuroda. Die Serieskomposition übernimmt Aki Mizuki (Risa Mizuno), während das Charakterdesign von Akira Ono stammt. Die Soundregie liegt bei Hozumi Gōda und die Musik komponiert Shiho Terada, während HIGHWAY STAR für die Musikproduktion verantwortlich ist. Weitere Informationen zum Starttermin sind bislang nicht bekannt.

In Japan erscheint die Reihe seit Januar 2018 im »Asuka«-Magazin von Kadokawa und umfasst mit dem heute veröffentlichten neuesten Band bislang 15 Bände. Seven Seas Entertainment veröffentlicht das Werk auf Englisch (13. Bände), während Altraverse die Reihe hierzulande unter »Koyo: Die exzentrische Ärztin des Mondblumenreiches« auf Deutsch verlegt (9. Bände).

📜 Beschreibung 》
Im Reich der Mondblume gilt die heilende Badekunst als bewährtes Mittel gegen Krankheiten und Verletzungen. Doch als Kronprinz Keiun während einer Inspektionsreise Zeuge wird, wie die junge Ärztin Koyō eine erstaunliche Wundnaht durchführt, gerät sein Blick auf die Medizin des Landes ins Wanken. Zwischen alten Überzeugungen, politischen Intrigen und tief verwurzelten Traditionen stellen sich die beiden den Problemen ihres Reiches.
